headline: Use one operator system for launch, implementation, and governed deployment
subheadline: myappai is designed for teams that want fewer handoffs between strategy, content, operations, payment routing, and deployment execution.
stepsHeadline: A better route from initial interest to shipped execution

steps.title: Clarify the commercial path
steps.description: The site now makes checkout, implementation, and enterprise qualification visibly distinct.

steps.title: Use the right operating surface
steps.description: Public pages handle demand and conversion while admin handles commands, deploys, and content control.

steps.title: Keep deploy state visible
steps.description: The release layer stays part of the system story instead of a hidden operational afterthought.

sections.title: For founder-led products
sections.description: Create a tighter path from offer idea to live page, payment lane, and deploy-ready execution.

sections.title: For agencies and service teams
sections.description: Use guided implementation to qualify work, package delivery, and support faster launches.

sections.title: For technical and enterprise teams
sections.description: Keep the repo-backed workflow, admin visibility, and enterprise deployment lane ready for more complex rollout requirements.

faq.question: Why is the buying model split into multiple lanes?
faq.answer: Because not every serious buyer wants the same path. Some want immediate software access, some want guided implementation, and some need a qualified rollout process.

faq.question: Why does deployment matter on a marketing site?
faq.answer: Because the public trust story is stronger when the same system that sells the work can also manage the release path.

cta.heading: Use pricing and products to move into the lane that fits your buying style.
cta.body: The redesign is built to remove confusion between product interest, implementation interest, and enterprise interest.
